commands.review_summary:
The PR strips redundant provider self-prefixes before runtime model normalization, preserves catalog/config prefix behavior, and adds regression coverage for affected providers and fallback candidates.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +38. Total +51 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main normalizes fallback candidates through normalizeModelRef and only Anthropic strips self-prefixes, so google/xai/openai/vercel self-prefixed refs can survive into provider calls.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because there is no concrete patch defect; maintainers should confirm the compatibility/auth-provider choice and wait for exact-head CI.

target.title: fix(models): strip remaining provider self prefixes
target.body:
## Summary
- Strip duplicate provider self prefixes in the agent model-ref path before provider calls.
- Keep static/config/catalog normalization prefix-preserving so persisted Google catalog refs stay as provider-qualified ids.
- Add regression coverage for self-prefixed fallback candidates and catalog-preserving normalization.

## Linked context
Closes #88770.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Google fallback/model selection no longer sends a self-prefixed model id such as `google/gemini-2.0-flash` to the Google GenerateContent endpoint, while config/catalog normalization still persists `google/gemini-*` refs.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout at exact head `40cb1d92c2ee31dab71a6d7044d3cdea96048b42`, Node 24.15.0, live Google Generative Language API with the Gemini key read from 1Password item `AI API Key - Google Gemini - GEMINI_API_KEY - steipete-m5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a live Node proof importing `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts`, normalizing provider `google` with configured model `google/gemini-2.0-flash`, then calling `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
pr88781_live_model_proof_begin
head=40cb1d92c2ee31dab71a6d7044d3cdea96048b42
provider=google
configuredModel=google/gemini-2.0-flash
normalizedModel=gemini-2.0-flash
doubledPrefix=absent
secretSource=1password:AI API Key - Google Gemini - GEMINI_API_KEY - steipete-m5
googleGenerateContentStatus=200
googleGenerateContentOk=true
responseTextRedacted=len:3;containsOk:true
pr88781_live_model_proof_end
pr88781_live_exit:0
```

Observed result after fix: The selected runtime model id is `gemini-2.0-flash`, the doubled `google/` prefix is absent, and the real Google API call succeeds with HTTP 200.

What was not tested: Other provider live APIs were not called; xAI/OpenAI coverage here is unit-level because the regression is shared model-ref normalization.

## Tests and validation
```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/pr88781-config-vitest-cache3 fnm exec --using=24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/config/io.write-prepare.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr88781-vitest-cache3 fnm exec --using=24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts
fnm exec --using=24.15.0 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.packages.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/pr88781-fix-test-packages.tsbuildinfo
fnm exec --using=24.15.0 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/pr88781-fix-test-src.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```
target.author: charles-openclaw
